data.title: Xinte Energy Overhauls Audit Oversight With New Board Committee
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1799) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. has established an Audit Committee under its board of directors to strengthen internal supervision, risk control, and corporate governance in line with PRC company law, Hong Kong Listing Rules, and its own articles of association. The committee will review financial information and disclosure, oversee internal and external audit work, and supervise internal controls, supported by the company’s audit department, which will handle preparatory and research tasks.

The Audit Committee will comprise five non-executive directors, with a majority being independent non-executive directors and at least one member possessing professional accounting or financial management expertise, and it bars recent partners of the external audit firm from membership for one year to safeguard independence. These measures are designed to enhance the integrity of financial oversight, improve transparency, and reinforce investor confidence in the company’s governance framework.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1799) stock is a Hold with a HK$8.00 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:1799 Stock Forecast page.

More about Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. Class H

Xinte Energy Co., Ltd. operates in the energy sector, with its securities listed on the Main Board of the Hong Kong Stock Exchange. The company is subject to the Company Law of the People’s Republic of China and Hong Kong Listing Rules, and it maintains a board structure that includes non-executive and independent non-executive directors to support corporate governance.

data.title: CMGE Narrows 2025 Loss as Overseas Gaming Revenue Surges Despite Flagship Miss
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

CMGE Technology Group Limited ( (HK:0302) ) has shared an announcement.

CMGE Technology Group reported 2025 revenue of RMB1.39 billion, down from the prior year, and a narrowed annual loss of RMB1.48 billion as its flagship cross-platform game Legend of Sword and Fairy: World underperformed expectations and weighed on production and operations. Despite the setback, the company improved operating results in the second half, driven by stricter cost discipline and a stronger performance from other titles.

Management accelerated overseas expansion, lifting overseas revenue 31.6% year-on-year to RMB358.2 million and raising its share to 25.8% of total sales as CMGE launched localized versions of multiple games across Japan, Greater China, Southeast Asia and Vietnam. In the Mainland China market, new IP-based releases such as Chunqiu Mystery, New Romance of the Three Kingdoms: The Legend of Cao Cao and Naruto: Konoha Masters exceeded or met expectations and contributed incremental revenue, underscoring the firm’s strategy to leverage proprietary and licensed IP to stabilize growth after the flagship miss.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:0302) stock is a Hold with a HK$0.37 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on CMGE Technology Group Limited stock, see the HK:0302 Stock Forecast page.

More about CMGE Technology Group Limited

CMGE Technology Group Limited is a China-based mobile and online game developer and publisher, focusing on cross-platform titles and the operation of well-known intellectual properties such as Legend of Sword and Fairy. The group develops and distributes games in Mainland China and is increasingly targeting overseas markets including Japan, Hong Kong, Macau, Taiwan, Southeast Asia and Vietnam to diversify revenue and expand its international footprint.

Average Trading Volume: 2,856,266

Technical Sentiment Signal: Sell

Current Market Cap: HK$736.9M

Anton Oilfield Services Group has declared a final ordinary dividend of RMB 0.0373 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, payable in renminbi to shareholders on 11 June 2026. Shareholders of Anton Oilfield Services Group must hold shares by the record date of 4 June 2026, with the stock trading ex-dividend on 29 May 2026 and the register closing from 2 to 4 June 2026. data.title: InSilico Medicine Strikes Multi-Billion-Dollar AI Drug Discovery Deal With Eli Lilly
data.description:
End of Quarter Sale - 50% Off TipRanks

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Discover top-performing stock ideas and upgrade to a portfolio of market leaders with Smart Investor Picks

InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo ( (HK:3696) ) has issued an update.

InSilico Medicine has entered a global pipeline licensing and AI drug discovery collaboration with Eli Lilly, leveraging its Pharma.AI engine to accelerate the discovery and development of novel oral therapeutics across multiple disease areas. Lilly receives an exclusive worldwide license to develop, manufacture and commercialize certain preclinical oral therapies, while the partners jointly run R&D programs on Lilly-selected targets, combining Insilico’s AI platforms with Lilly’s development and disease expertise.

Under the agreement, Insilico is entitled to a USD115 million upfront payment and could earn up to approximately USD2.75 billion in development, regulatory and commercial milestones, plus tiered royalties on future sales. The deal significantly enhances Insilico’s financial resources and validates its AI-driven drug discovery model, strengthening the company’s position as a key AI partner for large pharmaceutical groups and potentially accelerating the translation of its pipeline into commercially viable therapies.

More about InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo

InSilico Medicine Cayman TopCo is a Hong Kong-listed biotechnology company that integrates artificial intelligence and automation to accelerate drug discovery and advance life sciences. Its in-house capabilities and Pharma.AI platforms target unmet medical needs in fibrosis, oncology, immunology, pain, obesity and metabolic disorders, while also extending into advanced materials, agriculture, nutrition and veterinary medicine.

Anton Oilfield Services Group ( (HK:3337) ) just unveiled an update.

Anton Oilfield Services Group reported solid growth for 2025, with consolidated revenue rising 17.2% year on year to RMB5,571.7 million and net profit jumping 48.8% to RMB383.2 million, supported by stronger profitability and operating performance. Profit attributable to equity holders climbed 53.8% to RMB373.1 million, while net operating cash inflow and free cash flow both improved, and the board proposed a sharply higher final dividend of RMB112.0 million, signaling confidence in the group’s cash generation and rewarding shareholders with a 53.4% increase in payout over 2024.

The company’s stronger balance of earnings and cash flow suggests continued improvement in operational efficiency and financial resilience, despite higher working capital tied up in receivables and prepayments. By pairing profit growth with an enlarged dividend, Anton is reinforcing its market positioning as a growing yet increasingly shareholder-focused oilfield services player, which may bolster investor sentiment and support its competitive standing in a still cyclical energy services sector.

Top Spring International Holdings Limited ( (HK:3688) ) has provided an update.

Top Spring International Holdings Limited has warned that its net loss for the year ended 31 December 2025 is expected to widen to about HK$3.0 billion, compared with a net loss of approximately HK$1.9 billion a year earlier. The board highlighted that the deterioration stems mainly from fair value losses on investment properties, fair value losses on certain financial assets measured at fair value through profit or loss, and impairment losses on certain inventories.

The figures are based on unaudited consolidated management accounts and a preliminary board assessment, and may differ from the final audited annual results due to ongoing finalisation and review. The company plans to publish its audited annual results on 30 March 2026 and has cautioned shareholders and potential investors to exercise care and seek professional advice when dealing in its securities, underscoring heightened risk and uncertainty around its financial position.

China Financial Services Holdings Limited ( (HK:0605) ) has provided an update.

China Financial Services Holdings Limited reported a 7.9% decline in interest and financing consultancy services income to HK$93.6 million for the year ended 31 December 2025, but overall profitability improved sharply. Profit attributable to owners rose 73.3% to HK$71.5 million, and basic earnings per share increased to HK$0.35, driven largely by reversals of loan and interest payables and lower impairment losses compared with the prior year.

Despite higher interest and handling expenses and reduced core financing income, the Group benefited from HK$86.97 million in reversals of loan and interest payables and smaller write-downs on financial instruments, which offset general and administrative costs. The emergence of HK$10.2 million in education service income also contributed to the top line, suggesting an evolving business mix that may reduce reliance on its traditional financing consultancy segment and potentially support more resilient earnings going forward.

China Financial Services Holdings Limited is a Hong Kong-incorporated company listed on the Stock Exchange of Hong Kong, operating primarily in interest and financing consultancy services. The group has also begun generating income from education services, indicating a move to diversify its revenue streams beyond traditional financing-related activities.

Dahon Tech (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:2543) ) has shared an announcement.

Dahon Tech (Shenzhen) Co., Ltd. has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 1.118 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 14 May 2026. The dividend, to be paid in Hong Kong dollars with the exact amount and exchange rate to be announced, will have an ex-dividend date of 21 May 2026 and a payment date of 18 June 2026.

Wuxi Sunlit Science & Technology Co., Ltd. Class H ( (HK:1289) ) has issued an announcement.

Wuxi Sunlit Science and Technology Company Limited has declared a final ordinary cash dividend of RMB 0.08 per share for the financial year ended 31 December 2025, with shareholder approval scheduled for 26 June 2026. The dividend, to be paid in Hong Kong dollars at an exchange rate yet to be announced, will go ex-dividend on 30 June 2026, use 8 July 2026 as the record date, and be paid on 17 July 2026, signaling continued cash returns to shareholders and potentially reinforcing investor confidence in the company’s financial performance.

The Hong Kong share register will close from 3 July to 8 July 2026, with transfers required by the afternoon of 2 July 2026 to qualify for the payout, while details on applicable withholding tax are still pending. By maintaining a regular final dividend and clearly outlining key timetable dates for entitlement and payment, the company underscores its commitment to shareholder returns and transparent capital management, which could help its positioning in the Hong Kong capital market.

The most recent analyst rating on (HK:1289) stock is a Buy with a HK$2.50 price target. To see the full list of analyst forecasts on Wuxi Sunlit Science & Technology Co., Ltd. Class H stock, see the HK:1289 Stock Forecast page.

Wuxi Sunlit Science and Technology Company Limited, listed in Hong Kong under stock code 01289, operates in the science and technology sector and is incorporated as a Class H share issuer. The company serves investors in both mainland China and Hong Kong through its equity listing and regular cash dividend distributions.

China XLX Fertiliser Ltd. ( (HK:1866) ) just unveiled an update.

China XLX Fertiliser reported revenue of RMB25.35 billion for 2025, up from RMB23.13 billion a year earlier, driven by its core fertiliser operations despite higher costs. Gross profit edged down to RMB3.81 billion, while finance costs and operating expenses remained significant, reflecting a challenging cost environment.

Net profit dropped to RMB1.30 billion from RMB2.01 billion in 2024, with profit attributable to shareholders falling to RMB932 million and basic earnings per share declining to 76 RMB cents. Global New Material International Holdings Limited ( (HK:6616) ) has provided an update.

Global New Material International reported a sharp 76.9% jump in revenue to RMB2.92 billion for 2025, driven by strong sales growth, but rising costs and expenses squeezed profitability. Gross profit increased 30.5% to RMB1.14 billion, while adjusted EBITDA climbed 37.7%, yet higher selling, administrative and finance costs pushed the group from a RMB320.27 million profit in 2024 to a RMB306.36 million loss in 2025.

The company’s adjusted net profit fell 20.4% to RMB254.71 million, underlining the pressure from surging operating expenditures and financing expenses despite top-line expansion. Earnings per share swung from RMB0.19 to a basic and diluted loss per share of RMB0.31, signaling margin and leverage challenges that may concern shareholders even as the business scales its revenue base and maintains positive underlying cash-generating capacity.

Golden Throat Holdings Group Co Ltd ( (HK:6896) ) just unveiled an announcement.

Golden Throat Holdings reported a sharp deterioration in 2025 results, with revenue falling 20.4% year on year to RMB943.3 million and gross profit down 19.8% to RMB717.0 million. Earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation and amortisation dropped 22.9% to RMB377.1 million, while profit attributable to shareholders slid 21.2% to RMB251.2 million, translating into basic earnings per share of RMB33.98 cents versus RMB43.10 cents a year earlier.

Despite the downturn, the board is recommending a final dividend of HK$0.34 per share for 2025, subject to shareholder approval at the upcoming annual general meeting. The maintained payout underlines the company’s continued emphasis on shareholder returns, even as weaker sales and profitability may raise concerns about demand dynamics and cost pressures in its core healthcare consumer products business.
